serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is approximately 350 g, comprising one filled flour tortilla wrap with grilled chicken garlic sausage, vegetables, and garlic mayo, served alongside a portion of french fries.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken sausage, grilled; flour tortilla wrap; mayonnaise; mixed salad vegetables — composite estimation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Chicken Garlic Sausage Wrap exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Nutrient values estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data: grilled chicken sausage (FDC ID 172960), flour tortilla (FDC ID 325871), mayonnaise (FDC ID 172741), lettuce, capsicum, onion, and french fries (FDC ID 170720). Weighted composite per 100 g of assembled wrap (excluding fries in per-100g calculation; fries included in serving GL). Values represent a moderate-confidence estimate for this restaurant-style product.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of wheat flour tortilla wrap and mixed chicken sausage composite — analogous estimate based on flour tortilla GI (GI=52) from University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Chicken Garlic Sausage Wrap. GI estimated at 52 based on the dominant carbohydrate vehicle being a wheat flour tortilla wrap (GI=52, University of Sydney GI Database, Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283). The protein and fat content of the chicken sausage and garlic mayo further attenuate postprandial glucose response, supporting a low GI classification. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (52 × 76.6 g carbohydrate in 350 g serving at 21.5 g/100g) / 100 ≈ 39.8. gi_category set to 'low' per threshold rule (GI ≤ 55).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg/100 g; ~1820 mg per serving) from processed chicken sausage, garlic mayonnaise, and seasoning
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to its very high sodium content per serving (~1820 mg), which represents approximately 79% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg; request reduced-sodium preparation or consume only a half portion.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~39.8 per full serving) driven by refined wheat flour tortilla and french fries; partially offset by protein and fat content
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious with this meal due to its high glycemic load per serving (~39.8), primarily from the refined flour wrap and accompanying fries; consuming without fries and choosing a smaller portion can substantially reduce postprandial glucose excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~2.9 g/100 g) and high sodium from processed sausage and garlic mayonnaise; trans fat present at trace levels (~0.1 g/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this wrap due to its combination of moderate saturated fat, trace trans fat, and high sodium from the processed sausage and mayonnaise; opting for a grilled chicken breast alternative without processed sausage would significantly improve the cardiovascular risk profile.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Garlic Sausage Wrap is a fusion street-food-style dish popular in Singapore's Indian-influenced casual dining scene. It consists of a grilled chicken garlic sausage — a seasoned, processed poultry sausage with pronounced garlic flavour — wrapped in a soft wheat flour tortilla together with shredded lettuce, sliced capsicum (bell pepper), and onion rings. The wrap is dressed with garlic mayonnaise, adding a creamy, pungent richness. It is typically served with a side of french fries, making it a substantial meal. The texture profile combines the snap and juiciness of the grilled sausage with the softness of the tortilla, the crunch of fresh vegetables, and the creaminess of the mayo. Nutritionally, the dish provides moderate protein (~10.5 g/100 g) from the chicken sausage, moderate carbohydrate (~21.5 g/100 g) primarily from the refined flour wrap and fries, and moderate fat (~10.8 g/100 g) from the sausage and mayonnaise. Sodium content is high (~520 mg/100 g; ~1820 mg per full serving), making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low), though the glycemic load per full serving is substantial (~39.8) due to portion size, which is relevant for Type 2 Diabetes management. The dish is halal-certified in most Singapore outlets serving it. It is best consumed as an occasional meal rather than a daily staple, particularly for those monitoring sodium, saturated fat, or carbohydrate intake.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Chicken Garlic Sausage Wrap exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or USDA FoodData Central as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component-level data for grilled chicken sausage, flour tortilla, mayonnaise, and vegetables. GI is estimated from the closest analogous carbohydrate vehicle (wheat flour tortilla, GI=52) per the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central component entries; no single approved-source entry for this exact dish was found. Values should be treated as moderate-confidence estimates pending direct laboratory analysis or an exact HPB/ICMR match. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central component sources used for this composite estimate. 3. GI is estimated at 52 based on the wheat flour tortilla as the dominant carbohydrate vehicle (University of Sydney GI Database); gi_category is 'low' per the ≤55 threshold rule. 4. Glycemic load per serving (~39.8) is high despite low GI, due to the large serving size (350 g) and substantial carbohydrate from both the wrap and accompanying fries. 5. Sodium content is high and represents the primary clinical concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management. 6. The dish is classified as Indian cuisine per the source restaurant's cuisine tag, reflecting Singapore's Indian-influenced casual dining context. 7. Halal status is assumed based on the restaurant context (#Foodcoholic, Singapore Indian cuisine outlet) but should be verified against MUIS certification for the specific outlet. 8. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA component data and carry moderate uncertainty. 9. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the source database components used.
